entry.title: Join Us at ASUG Tech Connect 2024: Elevate Your SAP Journey with Exclusive Sessions
entry.content:
ASUG Tech Connect 2024 is just around the corner, offering an exciting opportunity to explore the latest SAP innovations, connect with industry leaders, and dive deep into sessions that will empower your SAP transformation. With insightful presentations and expert-led discussions, you'll gain practical insights to drive success in your organization. 

We are thrilled to announce a series of must-attend sessions hosted by our top executives and experts. Whether you're focusing on AI, cloud strategies, or system operations, these sessions will provide the knowledge and tools you need to optimize your SAP experience. Plus, don't miss the chance to visit our booth within the SAP area, which will be dedicated to SAP CloudALM and Customer Support.

Let’s help you transform your business.

Here’s what you can expect:

November 12, 2024 at 10:15 AM

Crafting an AI Roadmap: Strategies for Measuring AI Benefits
Speakers: Stefan Steinle and Wieland Schreiner
Join Stefan and Wieland as they delve into the intricacies of creating a robust AI roadmap tailored to your organization's needs. This session will cover essential strategies for planning and implementing AI initiatives while ensuring measurable benefits. Learn how to assess the impact of AI on your operations, optimize your processes, and quantify the value added, enabling a clear path to AI-driven success.

November 12, 2024 at 10:15 AM

Choosing the Right Path to SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Speaker: Jörg Marenk
Discover the various options SAP offers for transitioning to the cloud, including SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Private Edition, and two-tier scenarios. Explore which is the best fit for your organization and gain insights into the latest version, SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408.

November 12, 2024 at 11:15 AM

Support Expert Session: Harnessing AI and Automation for an Enhanced Support Experience
Speakers: Mohammed Ajouz, Oliver Huschke
Explore how to leverage AI and intelligent automation to maximize your support experience. In this session, discover how to use live support channels and preventative services to receive exceptional value and improve support outcomes. Learn about the latest AI enhancements, including Incident Solution Matching, automatic translation, and channel recommender available through SAP for Me and Built-in Support. Gain practical insights and tips from our experts to help you access the right support services more effectively. Leave with actionable strategies to make the most of your support interactions and achieve better results.

November 12, 2024 at 11:15 AM

Control SAP BTP Changes with SAP Cloud ALM: Best Practices and Observability
Speakers: Umesh Jagadesh, Evan Stoddard
Unlock the full potential of SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) with SAP Cloud ALM. Discover how to effectively manage and control changes in SAP BTP to drive efficiency and innovation. Learn best practices for SAP BTP operations, including the use of Terraform for automatic resource management, central monitoring with SAP Cloud ALM, and new administrative features for usage and cost monitoring. Explore how SAP Cloud ALM serves as the central observability platform for custom CAP applications, using OpenTelemetry for comprehensive monitoring across local and central tools.

November 12, 2024 at 2:25 PM

Transform Your SAP Experience: Unlocking the Power of Cloud and Support
Speakers: Geoff Scott, Stefan Steinle
Ready to revolutionize your SAP journey? Join Stefan and Geoff as they reveal how harmonizing cloud operations with customer support leads to seamless implementations. Discover the game-changing potential of Cloud ALM and RISE with SAP. Learn from real-world success stories and empower your organization to thrive in the cloud.

November 13, 2024 at 10:15 AM

SAP Runs SAP: Leading the AI Revolution in Customer Support
Speakers: Stefan Steinle, Jens Trotzky
Discover our AI-driven customer support model, where innovative strategies fuse AI with customer service to enhance efficiency and satisfaction. Explore our journey as we set new standards in AI-powered service excellence. Leverage this blueprint to seamlessly integrate AI into your organization and unlock success through the SAP for Me portal.

November 13, 2024 at 11:05 AM

Revolutionizing Operations: SAP Cloud ALM Best Practices for UiPath’s SAP S/4HANA Journey
Speakers: Sanjoy Rath, Jorge Ruiz
In the digital era, efficient process monitoring is crucial. UiPath excels by integrating with third-party products like Office365, MS Teams, Slack Channel, and other SaaS Products for real-time alerts and collaborative problem-solving.  Attend this session to hear how the integration of SAP Cloud ALM enhances UiPath's capabilities by providing comprehensive monitoring for RISE with SAP systems, including SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, and boundary systems.  

November 13, 2024 at 11:15 AM

How to Move from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM
Speakers: Evan Stoddard, Vijay Prakash
Transitioning from SAP Solution Manager to SAP Cloud ALM is crucial for enhancing implementations, operations, and service. This session will guide you through various adoption scenarios and provide actionable insights for planning and executing your transition. Learn about the tools and content available now, as well as what's on the product roadmap, to streamline your move to SAP Cloud ALM.

 

November 13, 2024 at 2:40 PM

Transform Your Business with an Integrated Transformation Toolchain
Speaker: Jörg Marenk
Discover how to navigate a clean core ERP transformation using an integrated business transformation toolchain with the RISE with SAP methodology. Learn how to effectively transform your business processes and application landscape while maintaining a lean environment. Explore how to utilize SAP LeanIX, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP Signavio solutions to drive your strategy from planning to execution, ensuring a clean core and long-term sustainability.

November 14, 2024 at 12:45 PM

Adopt Clean Core During your Conversion to SAP S/4HANA Cloud
Speakers: Wieland Schreiner, Jörg Marenk, Evan Stoddard
Discover how SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Readiness Check can enhance your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system conversion by offering robust planning, execution, and monitoring capabilities. Learn how to adopt a clean core ERP for a smooth transition.

November 14, 2024 at 4:15 PM

SAP for Me: Bundles Unleashed
Speakers: Anna Withum, Carsten Schwarz
The new "Bundles" feature empowers you to align your SAP systems seamlessly with your business processes. This innovative tool allows you to use your own terminology, ensuring you fully grasp the impact of any disruptions and clearly identify which processes are affected. Don't miss this opportunity to learn how "Bundles" can enhance your operational efficiency and provide you with greater clarity and control. Join us to explore this feature in depth and discover how it can benefit your organization.

These sessions are packed with actionable insights and hands-on strategies to help you maximize your SAP investment. Make sure to add these sessions to your personal agenda and visit us at our booth in the SAP area, where we’ll be showcasing SAP CloudALM and discussing how our customer support solutions can transform your SAP journey.

Register now and take the next step in optimizing your SAP operations at ASUG Tech Connect 2024!

entry.title: #04 - Launch Your Project: The Broader Perspective Digital Transformation
entry.content:
Transforming your business with cloud technology calls for a paradigm shift in the way you work, think, plan, and execute. This is an exciting and rewarding journey, which requires the right strategy, effort, and investments to get all your people on the same page. Collaboration across different teams and experts is essential to ensure a successful project and to achieve the outcomes you set out for the organization.

A move to the cloud is more than just a software implementation, automating how the business works. It involves changing and adapting core – often long-standing – processes and workflows to meet changing business goals, remain competitive, and meet customer demands. It is about an innovative and business focused approach to redefine how work is done so that it helps the organization succeed. Technology is probably THE most important enabler of the transformation NOT the transformation per se.

The potential of Business Transformation, which implementing any cloud technology brings, will only be determined by establishing a future to present strategy and the efforts being made to drive it.

Enabling Business Transformation

According to the recommendations of many global organizations that already call themselves “successful” on their cloud enabled transformation plus the studies from other reputable sources we say that to enable meaningful Business Transformation results organizations are required to activate the right strategies and programs to accompany their cloud technology implementation and sustainment efforts.

Amazing business results are possible but do not come like magic just because an organization acquires SuccessFactors. “Magic” is the a result of having the right technologies, a future to present mindset and defining the right transformation programs and resources to your organization’s needs.

The below Business Transformation Framework highlights the various programs to help with a successful Business Transformation. 

Copyright - Customer Transformation Advisory, SAP SuccessFactors

The Business Transformation Framework encompasses 11 important elements that need to play altogether to achieve expected results:

1. Business Transformation Foundation:

The Foundational elements of the Business Transformation Framework, clarify the purpose of the transformation focusing on the Strategic elements which need to be well defined and communicated. This ensures the direction is well set and all actions should focus on achieving desired outcomes and business goals, which tie up with your overall business strategy and which provide the guiding principles for the way technology is configured and integrated:

1.1. Strategy and Digital Plan:

Establish the future direction and business value focusing on key priorities, goals, and outcomes for the overall business but specially for the business area that is adopting the new technology.  A successful Digital Strategy Plan considers the strategy of the specific business area and then defines where, when and how the use of technology would be essential to drive tangible results.

1.2. Organization and Operating Model:

Define the right Organizational Structure with value-add programs, processes, roles and skills. Does the organization and/or the business area need to revamp itself to deliver value to the internal and external stakeholders? What are the specific skill sets required?  It is best to adopt a “future to present mindset” when designing a model to execute programs and processes for the business with the right people and structure, enabled through a cloud-based solution.

1.3. Stakeholder Experience:

Design the processes to capture the future expectations of your stakeholders what do we want them to feel and sense on their journey?. Focus on the “moments that matter”. Involve your stakeholders in the implementation process earlier on, to help ensure user satisfaction and adoption, and avoid resistance and conflict. To ensure you are continuously reinforcing change and adoption, continue to think from the point of view of your personas and what matters to them.  

2. Operational Excellence:

This refers to the Tactical Programs that need to be in place on a continuous fashion to drive the transformation journey. These define the actions required to achieve your business goals and targets. This set of initiatives should be managed by a core dedicated team and supported by the business defined stakeholders. Let us look at these in detail.

2.1. Executive Sponsorship:

Having an executive sponsor lends credibility to transformation projects and highlights their strategic importance within the organization. It also ensures that resources as needed will be in place and obstacles will be removed or at least reduced at some point. Securing the right sponsor ensures strong leadership support, aids in communicating initiatives, securing necessary resources and budgets, and ensures full organizational buy-in.

2.2. Cloud Culture:

Organizations that embrace a cloud culture focus not only on their technological infrastructure but also on transforming their work processes. They invest in cultivating a new mindset and approach to technology where people is open and prepared to work with technology. To fully leverage the benefits of the cloud, fostering the right attitudes and behaviors among stakeholders is essential. This forms the foundation for successfully achieving business transformation goals.

2.3. Change Management:

The goal of change management is to implement a structured, continuous program that helps people in the organization transition from its current state to a desired future state. This program should focus on people, understanding the target personas, their behaviors, and what motivates them. It should remain relevant even after go-live. A key element is to address the three Vs—Vision, Value, and Vested Interest—of everyone involved and affected by the change through different initiatives as communication, training and sensitization.

According to an SAP Study, employees are more likely to adopt solutions if they help them achieve their goals. The image below illustrates the 3Ws for building trust in technology and encouraging better adoption among your workforce:

2.4. Governance Model:

Designing a program that fosters effective decision-making, ensures organizational alignment, and mitigates risks, should be a priority when designing any transformation project's Governance Model. A strong governance framework is essential for managing technology responsibly and efficiently. The model should begin with a clear purpose statement, include guiding principles, and outline specific goals and outcomes, with defined accountability and ownership for each decision.

Additionally, here’s a sample template of a Model Charter for an Executive Committee within an organization.

2.5. Sustainment:

Establish a program to manage and sustain both processes and technology solutions. Technology is never implemented solely to address current challenges; it's always an investment with the future in mind. Sustainment includes planning for ongoing support, adoption strategies, release management, and innovation management. During and after implementation, it's crucial to formally define the roles, responsibilities, and processes required to keep your solution updated, relevant, and continuously delivering business value. A simple RACI matrix can be a good starting point to monitor daily tasks, defining who is Accountable (Owner), Responsible (Doer), Consulted (as needed), and Informed (as required). It is very important to evolve the roles, responsibilities and processes identified during the implementation phase once Live with your solution in order to keep it updated, relevant and producing continued value and desired outcomes.

2.6. Value Management:

Establishing an approach to identify, track, and measure both quantitative and qualitative transformation outcomes is essential. The value management framework focuses on defining the metrics that align with our goals and objectives. It's important to identify and agree on the business KPIs that will drive the most significant outcomes, track them consistently, and measure progress against expectations

2.7. Optimization and Innovation:

This dimension refers to building an approach to advance people, processes, and technology transformation. With Cloud products, innovation is rapid and so should be their adoption. To enable agility under this dimension of the Business Transformation Framework, focus on activities such as evaluating optimization vs innovation within your organization. Innovation introduces new ideas and emerging technology into an existing product, process or service whilst optimization adds incremental improvements to an existing product, process or service. Another focus area should be the ideation of a new strategy which ties in with that of the overall business’ in the future, and with that, make sure to reimagine the programs that are going to be needed to support this new strategy. Conduct a business and functional alignment refresh by ensuring to update common goals, which will serve as the north star for the future direction of current projects and/or sustainment efforts and communicate these transparently. Finally, establish an innovation roadmap by creating a visual representation that aligns the organization’s strategy with innovation actions and brings clarity to the direction, processes, priorities, and timeline required to achieve specific goals. 

2.8. Technology Framework:

Align organizational principles for digital transformation and define tactics to boost adoption and usability for a better experience. The successful implementation of the project would have a lot of technological considerations, like Data Management, aligning with other transformation initiatives within the organization, Technology Roadmap, Network Devices and Mobile functionality. The team members who play a role in implementing the solution, need to make educated and informed decisions. This would mean, the teams to develop strong competencies according to their role, for them to be aware of the Best/ Leading Practices and be able to learning from the proven success of previous implementations and avoiding pitfalls.

3. Technology as the “Enabler”:

Technology will then be the FOUNDATION to support your business strategy Technology will act as both a driver of innovation and a tool for execution. Companies can leverage market leading technology to create digital ecosystems that bring together customers, suppliers, and partners, employee, improving collaboration and creating new value for everyone in the value chain. By aligning technological investments and implementations with business goals, companies can drive innovation, optimize processes, improve customer engagement, and ultimately, create sustainable competitive advantages.

Check out some helpful resources to further aid you with your Business Transformation journey!

  • Learn how organizational change management can contribute to the success of your SAP cloud project through the learning journey Discover Organizational Change Management for SAP Cloud Project and the Change Management Roadmap - Roadmap Viewer (sap.com)
  • Are you implementing any of the SAP SuccessFactors HCM solutions? Register for our Road to Adoption Webcast series to better understand how organizations are making changes to drive adoption and successful business outcomes.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series: Customer Onboarding at SAP.
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the authors:

  • Aarthi Iyer is an SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding Advisor and Events Lead, and her current role is to guide SuccessFactors customers through a successful implementation journey. With experience across various HCM roles, she has led multiple end-to-end implementation projects, architecting effective solutions for customers. Her expertise lies in guiding clients through successful HR transformation projects and helping them unlock long term value from their investments.
  • Vanessa Alvarado, 8+ years at SAP working as Global Customer Transformation Advisor and before that as CEE based in Mexico. Passionate for supporting customers all around the world to better define their transformation strategy enabled by HXM technology.
  • Nikki Pardo is a Senior Customer Onboarding Advisor within the SAP HCM Customer Onboarding Team, providing guidance, enablement, and services focused on developing HCM Customers’ internal experts to deliver successful implementations and achieve their key business goals. With more than 16 years of experience as a HR business professional and another 6 within the SuccessFactors field, Nikki’s interest in people, efficiencies, new technologies and organisational development allow her to expand on her passion for helping companies and individuals overcome the psychological and operational obstacles that stand in the way of reaching their full potential.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @aarthiiyer , @VaneA_Transformation or @narkixepardo.

entry.title: Learn how to get an S-user ID
entry.content:
SAP S-user ID, or S-user, is a unique identifier that allows you to login to your SAP products, report a case, download software, configure connections, and more. Your S-user ID contains the letter ‘S’ followed by a string of numbers (e.g. S00*******). 

You may come across the term ‘Super administrator’ and wonder how that differs from S-user. The super administrator within the SAP product your company is using (e.g. SAP Analytics Cloud) encompasses all administrative duties and has t highest level of authorization. The Super administrator is usually the person in your organization who originally purchased the SAP product. 

 

How are S-user IDs assigned? 

For new customers, the individual who purchased the SAP product will receive a welcome email containing their S-user ID. In this case, SAP assigns the first S-user ID. 

 

 The Super administrator can assign S-user IDs to various people in their organization, such as managers, who may need admin-level status. Everyone else in the company who uses the product does not necessarily need to have S-user IDs, they can simply login using their work email address once the Super administrator or S-user grants them access. 

 

 Please note, SAP cannot assign additional S-user IDs on your company’s behalf. 
 
If your organization has only one Super Administrator who has left, please contact the Customer Interaction Center to nominate a new Super Administrator. Ensure to provide the following information: 
 

 Ensure that the new nominee has below details: 

S-user ID (if already created): 

First name: 

Surname: 

Email address (note- domain of the email id should match company name): 

Phone number with country code: 

Designation: 

Reason for new super administrator nomination. 

 
To ensure that a new Super Admin can be nominated and granted Super Admin rights, please confirm that your organization has a valid maintenance agreement with SAP and that the installation is active. 

 

Let’s look at the steps, as how the company’s super admin creates an S-user ID. 

  1. Logon to SAP for Me and go to User Management  
  2. Click Request User on the top of the application 

     3. Fill in the Request User form 

  1. Click Submit 
  2. User was requested, click OK 
  • The user request is not instant and may take some time to complete. 
  • The new user will receive an e-mail from SAP Notification Services with the new S-user ID and an activation link, which is valid for 10 days. 
  • You can check the status of the new user on the Requested Users tab. Once the user is complete it will move to the Users tab. 
  • After the user is created, click the user ID and add a phone number or change any available details if needed. 
  • Assign authorizations to the user ID (see KBA 1511008). 

 

 

How to delete S-user IDs 

  1. In the User Management application, go to the Users tab 
  2. Scroll down the list or use the filter feature to find the S-user ID(s) to be deleted 
  3. Click the checkbox in front of the user(s) 
  4. Click Delete (if an error appears around important functions see KBA 1271517) 

 

 

  • It may take up to 24 hours to process your request for deletion. 
  • Deleted S-user IDs are listed in the Deleted Users tab of the application for twelve months and cannot be reactivated (KBA 1811859). 
  • Only a user that holds Super Administrator rights can delete another Super Administrator. 
  • S-user ID with important function (or critical roles) can be deleted only once those functions have been transferred to another S-user ID within the same company ERP number (KBA 1271517). 
  • Every customer account requires at least one Super Administrator. Therefore, you cannot delete all Super Administrators under your account. In exceptional cases when there is no active maintenance linked to your account, all Super Administrators can be deleted by SAP, provided you Account Executive confirms the request. 

For issues or questions on this topic, report a case for component XX-SER-FORME. Be sure to include details, S-user ID, screenshots, etc. to assist in troubleshooting. 

  • Online help for User, Authorization, and Administrator Concept 
  • KBA 2443869 - S-user ID authorization guide - SAP for Me 
  • KBA 1511008 - How to add or change S-user ID authorizations - SAP for Me 
  • KBA 1282854 - Information on adding/editing/deleting authorizations for S-user IDs - SAP for Me 
  • KBA 1614304 - How to use the Copy User's Authorization feature for S-user IDs - SAP for Me 

 

 

And now let’s check on how you can find the Super Admin details of your company 

  • Just go to me.sap.com and then >’Users &Contacts’ > My Important Contacts and you will see the list of all the S-users with special roles for each installation. 

 

If you want to make changes in the list of Important Contacts, please see these KBAs: 
 
How to maintain Super, Cloud, and User Administrator S-user IDs listed in My Important Contacts - SAP for Me 

How to change Software Recipient and Technical Contact users for on-premise installations listed in My Important Contacts - SAP for Me 

Thanks for reading!   
  
We would love to hear from you, let us know what you think? 😊 

 If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community   

 

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: How to reach out to SAP Education - Learning Hub team for support
entry.content:
Are you looking to get support from the Learning Hub/Training Team, and you do not know where to start? Then this blog is for you… 

Here I will share the steps that you need to follow to engage with the SAP Learning Support Center; the Support agents are available 24 hours per day, Monday to Friday and most queries are responded to within 24 hours. 

* Click here if you wish to read this in German, Italian or Spanish

This blog was created in collaboration with my colleague Alba Blasco

You can also check this video: Engaging with the SAP Learning Support Center

In the support platform you can also find FAQs related to: 

  • General Support Topics 
  • Practice Systems 
  • Training Reseller Partner Topics 
  • Learning and Certification Advise  
  • Course Booking and Payment  
  • And Analytics Dashboard 

How to create a case 

If you find that the information that you are looking for is not in the support platform, then you can create a case to get support on your specific query.  

All you need to do is to login with your Universal ID and create a ticket. 

From the SAP Learning Hub site: 

  1. You need to click on the question mark (?) icon found on the upper right side of the page. 
  2. In the Help Center page click on the 'Contact Us' button and choose 'Request Technical Assistance'. 
  3. Click on "Need More Help" 
  4. Login with your Universal ID and you then need to click on "Create Case" 

From the SAP Learning site: 

  1. Scroll down to the bottom of the page and click on 'Get Support' found under Support and Contact. 
  2. You will be directed to the next page, the User Support Center page, you can find the FAQs for self-help and detailed guides. 
  3. If the needed support is not found in the FAQs, kindly scroll down and click on the 'Need Help' icon and you will be directed to the next page to raise a ticket. 
  4. Click on the 'Create Case' button. 
     

Additionally, you can use the direct link to User Support Center. 

To view your ticket update: Please click on the refresh button before to find your case/ticket update. 

What can Support Agents assist you with? 

The Learning Team can assist you if you want to get information about course, certification content and learning journeys also they can advise you to understand what learning is needed to achieve your aims. 

Other services include:  

  • 1st Level questions on certification, including advice on correct UID set up and how you can access to your badges.  
  • Issues related to information, delivery, and after-care service of Instructor-Led classrooms. 
  • Bookings and billing related issues are usually handled by other internal team, but the Learning team can get involved to give approval for any free of charge re-bookings and similar scenarios as these are often related to customer complaints etc. 
  • Visa letters. 
  • Providing supplier vendor information on request. 

Request support in a different language. 

If you want the Learning team to assist you in your preferred language, you can select the language once you have added the description, you will need to pick the Country you are located and select from: German, English, Spanish, French and Japanese. 

We would also like to share this blog so you can learn more about SAP Certifications and UID...all you need to know! 

And this KBA can also be helpful: 2404504 - How to get help with the SAP Learning Hub or SAP Training Center

Thank you for reading!

entry.title: #05 - Understanding Project Methodology: The SAP Activate Methodology
entry.content:
The Importance of project management:

Effective project management is vital for delivering projects on time, within scope, and within budget. It provides structure and direction, helping teams navigate complex tasks and ensuring that everyone is aligned with the project's goals. Without a clear project management framework, projects can easily fall victim to scope creep, missed deadlines, and cost overruns.

Some common project management methodologies include:

  • Waterfall: A linear and sequential approach where each phase depends on the deliverables of the previous one.

  • Agile: An iterative approach that allows for flexibility and continuous improvement, often used in software development.

  • Scrum: A subset of Agile, focused on delivering smaller pieces of a project in "sprints" or time-boxed intervals.

  • Lean: A methodology that focuses on delivering maximum value to the customer by minimizing waste.

Each of these methodologies has its strengths, and the choice of methodology often depends on the nature of the project and the organization’s needs.

Introducing the SAP Activate Methodology:

At SAP, we’ve introduced a specialized project management methodology known as the SAP Activate Methodology. This methodology is designed specifically for SAP implementations and is tailored to meet the unique needs of businesses undergoing digital transformation.

What is the SAP Activate Methodology?

The SAP Activate Methodology is a simple, modular, and agile methodology used for implementing SAP solutions. It aims to accelerate implementations and drive continuous innovation by providing a standardized but flexible approach using best practices, agile principles, and a phased approach.

Key characteristics of the SAP Activate Methodology are:

  • Business Ownership: The business owns the solution, ensuring that the project aligns closely with business goals and delivers real value.

  • Lead with ‘Standard’ Best Practices: SAP Activate promotes using standard, pre-configured solutions that are based on SAP's best practices, minimizing the need for customization.

  • Agile, Modular, Scalable: The methodology supports an agile, scalable approach that can be adapted to different project sizes and complexities.

  • Rapid, Repeatable Delivery Steps: SAP Activate focuses on delivering value quickly and efficiently through repeatable steps.

  • Accelerators: Tools, templates, and content are provided to speed up the implementation process.

  • Quicker ROI: By using a fit-to-standard approach and best practices, organizations can achieve quicker return on investment.

  • SAP Solutions: designed to work with all SAP solutions, not just S/4HANA.

  • Six core project phases: Discover, Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy and Run.

Phases and main activities of the SAP Activate Methodology:

Access the SAP Activate Methodology tasks and accelerators via the SAP Roadmap Viewer:

The SAP Roadmap Viewer is a tool that provides access to SAP Activate Methodology implementation roadmaps. It offers a comprehensive view of project teams' activities, deliverables, and tasks associated with SAP implementations in in a structured way to support project planning and execution. It includes accelerator assets in document and hyperlink format to support implementation projects. The roadmaps are organized by logical categories or by SAP solution.

Recommendations for Success with the SAP Activate Methodology:

To fully leverage the SAP Activate Methodology, consider the following recommendations:

  • Learn about the SAP Activate Methodology: start with the Learning Journey Discovering SAP Activate Implementation Tools and Methodology to get a comprehensive understanding of how to manage your SAP projects using this methodology.

  • Join the SAP Activate Community: Engage with SAP Activate Methodology experts by joining the SAP Activate Community. This platform allows you to ask questions, share insights, and stay up-to-date with the latest news and developments in SAP Activate.

  • Explore the SAP Roadmap Viewer: The SAP Roadmap Viewer provides detailed guidance for your digital transformation journey. You can view and download SAP Activate Methodology assets and accelerators and even provide feedback to SAP.

  • Adapt to your project situation: While the SAP Activate Methodology provides a robust framework, it’s important to adapt the methodology to your specific project situation. Trust the process, but be flexible enough to make adjustments as needed to ensure the best outcomes.

Conclusion:

Understanding and implementing the right project methodology is crucial to the success of your SAP project. The SAP Activate Methodology offers a powerful, business-driven approach that aligns closely with best practices and the needs of modern businesses. By following the SAP Activate Methodology, engaging with the community, and utilizing available resources, you can ensure that your project is not only successful but also delivers rapid and sustainable value to your organization.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Check out other blogs from the series   Customer Onboarding at SAP
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author:

  • Inês Moura supports with the design and orchestration of the customer onboarding journey for SAP RISE and IAM (Intelligent Asset Management) customers, ensuring a seamless experience from signature to successful go-live. As an Onboarding Advisor, Inês provides expert guidance and enablement, facilitating a smooth transition from the sales cycle to project start. With a focus on targeted project guidance and risk mitigation, Inês is dedicated to supporting SAP customers throughout the entire onboarding journey.

Having questions, comments or feedbacks? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @InesMoura or @Linh-Hoang .
